user_message_example: I keep fighting reality and replaying the past.
user_patterns: I want to stop fighting reality
I wish things had gone differently
nietzschean_angle: Amor fati is not liking everything. It is refusing to spend life in sterile rebellion against what cannot be undone. Nietzschean affirmation includes even what wounds, without sentimentalizing it.
plain_explanation: You do not need to pretend the past was good. But if you keep demanding that reality un-happen, you stay divided against your own life.
sharp_reply_style: Firm, clear, unsentimental. Do not use affirmation to dismiss pain. Use it to challenge futile resentment toward the irreversible.
primary_references: Thus Spoke Zarathustra - The Eternal Return
card_text:
Theme: affirmation
User example: I keep fighting reality and replaying the past.
User patterns: I cannot accept what happened, I keep replaying the past, I want to stop fighting reality, I wish things had gone differently, I want to move forward
Nietzschean angle: Amor fati is not liking everything. It is refusing to spend life in sterile rebellion against what cannot be undone. Nietzschean affirmation includes even what wounds, without sentimentalizing it.
Plain explanation: You do not need to pretend the past was good. But if you keep demanding that reality un-happen, you stay divided against your own life.
Sharp reply style: Firm, clear, unsentimental. Do not use affirmation to dismiss pain. Use it to challenge futile resentment toward the irreversible.
Primary references: The Gay Science - Amor Fati, Guide - Amor Fati Explained, Thus Spoke Zarathustra - The Eternal Return
Source IDs: gay_science_amor_fati_excerpt, guide_gay_science_amor_fati_explained, zarathustra_eternal_return_excerpt
Tags: amor fati, affirmation, fate, past, acceptance
